description: SECOND REAL-INCIDENT TRACE (from the predecessor, AUDIT-003). The 2026-07-06 'bar night' blackout: protection went silently dark for 1h45m (gap_s=6620) at low battery - optical HR froze, then zeroed, and neither wearer nor guardian was ever told (predecessor KNOWN_LIMITATIONS L1). Representable half in Phase 0: a heart-rate stream, an explicit gap marker, 6620s of nothing, then resumption. Expects: unknown-widening on the gap (confidence 0, channels unknown - D13, VV-105 direction), NO false escalation from absence, and the gap visible in the decision log (absence must be evidence, never silence). The other half - guardian-facing coverage alerting/dead-man - is Phase 6+ (Coverage Monitor / ADR-106); this trace keeps that hole visible in evidence until then (D20).

provenance.source: AUDIT-003 appendix, sub-report 4 (history mining): predecessor KNOWN_LIMITATIONS L1, 2026-07-06 20:15:47->22:01 IST, gap_s=6620, stuck_hr bpm=77->102->0 at 15%->10% battery; no on-watch or guardian-facing indication
provenance.whyExists: The worst silent-coverage-loss incident the predecessor recorded: the system was dark for 105 minutes and nobody knew. The new pipeline must make absence itself first-class evidence.
provenance.whatItGuards: D13 (absence never defaults to normal) / H-04 (silent coverage loss) / VV-105 unknown-widening; documents the Phase-6 Coverage/dead-man gap until ADR-106 lands
